creator: eadler@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Sat, Nov 16, 2013 at 11:45 AM, Juanitou <listjm@club-internet.fr> wrote:
>> Related to nspluginwrapper specifically: can you try to reinstall it
>> now? There was a brief period of time when it was installing the
>> plugin to the wrong place.
>
>
> Sure! I deinstalled both nspluginwrapper and linux-f10-flasplugin11,
> reinstalled again and ran:
> % nspluginwrapper -v -a -i
>
> It installed the plugin and Firefox recognized it. Problem solved as long=
 as
> the plugin is reinstalled. On the other hand, if I understand it well, ju=
st
> updating those two ports with e.g. portmaster will break the functionalit=
y
> in Firefox. That=E2=80=99s what I did at first, the typical weekly updati=
ng of
> ports.

nspluginwrapper -v -a -u is required after every flash update.  I just
added a mention of this to the port's pkg-message.


--=20
Eitan Adler
